title: ground truth for a benchmark item
description: Held separately from the item so the public split can withhold it from methods under test. Ground truth is established independently of any solver: for a violated item the witness is a concrete input or trace that, when executed against the rule set, exhibits the violation, and this execution does not use a solver. For a holds item the basis records how the absence of any violation was established.

item.description: id of the benchmark item this key answers

witness.description: for a violated item: the concrete assignment (decision rule set) or event trace (transition system) that exhibits the violation. null for a holds item. The witness is replayable by concrete execution without a solver, which is what breaks the circularity of using a solver to label a benchmark that scores solvers.

seeded.description: true if the violation was deliberately introduced as a rule-interaction fault

seeded_bug.description: for a seeded item, the description of the introduced fault and the interacting rules that produce it

interaction_depth.description: number of distinct rules that must interact to produce the violation; 0 for a holds item; the compositionality hypothesis predicts probabilistic methods miss higher-depth violations

basis.description: how ground truth was established. by_construction: the fault was inserted and its witness recorded; concrete_replay: the witness was executed against the rule set and the violation observed; manual_proof: a holds item argued by hand or exhaustive enumeration independent of the system under test
